headline: Malaysia's Carbon Tax Proposal Shelved for Third Time
context: The Ministry of Finance confirmed in January 2026 that the proposed carbon tax, first floated in 2021, has been deferred again to 2028 following industry lobbying.

cards.big: Malaysia committed to 45% emissions reduction by 2030 at COP26.
cards.sub: The primary fiscal instrument to achieve it has been deferred 3 times in 5 years.

cards.big: Singapore implemented a carbon tax in 2019 at SGD 5 per tonne.
cards.sub: Malaysia's proposed rate of RM25 per tonne was reduced to RM15 after FMM objections — then shelved entirely.

cards.big: The EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism begins full enforcement in 2026.
cards.sub: Malaysian exporters face estimated tariffs of RM2.8B annually without a domestic carbon price.

cards.big: 7 rounds of stakeholder consultations produced no consensus.
cards.sub: Each round expanded the exemption list. The final draft exempted 62% of industrial emissions.

cards.big: A carbon tax that exempts 62% of emissions is not a climate policy. It is a press release.
The choice is not whether Malaysian industry pays a carbon price, but whether it pays Malaysia or the EU. Delay is itself a decision.
